corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In this paper, we systematically study neu- ral network architecture design choices for object detection and propose several key optimizations to improve efficiency.

context_window.previous: Model efficiency has become increasingly important in computer vision.
context_window.current: In this paper, we systematically study neu- ral network architecture design choices for object detection and propose several key optimizations to improve efficiency.
context_window.next: First, we propose a weighted bi-directional feature pyra- mid network (BiFPN), which allows easy and fast multi- scale feature fusion; Second, we propose a compound scal- ing method that uniformly scales the resolution, depth, and width for all backbone, feature network, and box/class pre- diction networks at the same time.

location.sentence: To address this issue, we propose to add an additional weight for each input, and let the network to learn the importance of each input feature.

location.sentence: Our intuition is simple: if a node has only one input edge with no feature fusion, then it will have less contribution to feature network that aims at fusing different features.

context_window.previous: To improve model efficiency, this paper proposes several optimizations for cross-scale connections: First, we remove those nodes that only have one input edge.
context_window.current: Our intuition is simple: if a node has only one input edge with no feature fusion, then it will have less contribution to feature network that aims at fusing different features.
context_window.next: This leads to a simplified bi- directional network; Second, we add an extra edge from the original input to output node if they are at the same level, in order to fuse more features without adding much cost; Third, unlike PANet [26] that only has one top-down and one bottom-up path, we treat each bidirectional (top-down & bottom-up) path as one feature network layer, and repeat the same layer multiple times to enable more high-level fea- ture fusion.

top_candidates.sentence: Figure shows the overall architecture of EfficientDet, which largely follows the one-stage detectors paradigm .
For simplicity, all accuracy results here are for COCO validation set.
In this paper, we systematically study neural network architecture design choices for object detection and propose several key optimizations to improve efficiency.
In this paper, we systematically study neural network architecture design choices for object detection and propose several key optimizations to improve efficiency.
In this paper, we systematically study neural network architecture design choices for object detection and propose several key optimizations to improve efficiency.

First, we propose a weighted bi-directional feature pyramid network (BiFPN), which allows easy and fast multiscale feature fusion; Second, we propose a compound scaling method that uniformly scales the resolution, depth, and width for all backbone, feature network, and box/class prediction networks at the same time.

In particular, with singlemodel and single-scale, our EfficientDet-D7 achieves stateof-the-art 55.1 AP on COCO test-dev with 77M parameters and 410B FLOPs 1 , being 4x -9x smaller and using 13x -42x fewer FLOPs than previous detectors.
For example, the latest AmoebaNet-based NAS-FPN detector requires 167M parameters and 3045B FLOPs (30x more than RetinaNet ) to achieve state-ofthe-art accuracy.
In this paper, we mainly follow the one-stage detector design, and we show it is possible to achieve both better efficiency and higher accuracy with optimized network architectures.
In this paper, we mainly follow the one-stage detector design, and we show it is possible to achieve both better efficiency and higher accuracy with optimized network architectures.
More recently, NAS-FPN leverages neural architecture search to automatically design feature network topology.
More recently, NAS-FPN leverages neural architecture search to automatically design feature network topology.
Although it achieves better performance, NAS-FPN requires thousands of GPU hours during search, and the resulting feature network is irregular and thus difficult to interpret.
Although it achieves better performance, NAS-FPN requires thousands of GPU hours during search, and the resulting feature network is irregular and thus difficult to interpret.

In order to obtain better accuracy, it is common to scale up a baseline detector by employing bigger backbone networks (e.g., from mobile-size models and ResNet , to ResNeXt and AmoebaNet ), or increasing input image size (e.g., from 512x512 to 1536x1536 ).
Some recent works show that increasing the channel size and repeating feature networks can also lead to higher accuracy.
In this section, we ablate various design choices for our proposed EfficientDet.

In this paper, we systematically study network architecture design choices for efficient object detection, and propose a weighted bidirectional feature network and a customized compound scaling method, in order to improve accuracy and efficiency.

In this paper, we systematically study network architecture design choices for efficient object detection, and propose a weighted bidirectional feature network and a customized compound scaling method, in order to improve accuracy and efficiency.

In this paper, we systematically study network architecture design choices for efficient object detection, and propose a weighted bidirectional feature network and a customized compound scaling method, in order to improve accuracy and efficiency.

In this paper, we systematically study network architecture design choices for efficient object detection, and propose a weighted bidirectional feature network and a customized compound scaling method, in order to improve accuracy and efficiency.

Based on these optimizations, we develop a new family of detectors, named EfficientDet, which consistently achieve better accuracy and efficiency than the prior art across a wide spectrum of resource constraints.
In particular, our scaled EfficientDet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with much fewer parameters and FLOPs than previous object detection and semantic segmentation models.
In particular, our scaled EfficientDet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with much fewer parameters and FLOPs than previous object detection and semantic segmentation models.
In particular, our scaled EfficientDet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with much fewer parameters and FLOPs than previous object detection and semantic segmentation models.
